test_cases.description: Labor/economics newsletter and podcast — The Margin — documentary journalism visual position
test_cases.input_summary: Clear editorial personality, named references (Walker Evans, NYT Magazine), specific color direction, strong anti-references provided
test_cases.output_summary: Visual position statement is specific and evocative; five dimensions in prose (not bullet points); Walker Evans reference citation specifies 'forensic precision not nostalgic quotation'; AI prompt anchor is functional and usable; 'what this is not' section is specific about three failure modes

test_cases.description: Science education YouTube channel — bright, curious, accessible — no prior visual identity
test_cases.input_summary: New project, no existing references, general 'bright and curious' direction, YouTube primary platform, young-adult audience
test_cases.output_summary: Skill generates reference suggestions when user hasn't provided them (Kurzgesagt for animated context, National Geographic for photography context); five dimensions reflect the curious-optimistic register without defaulting to generic educational imagery; AI anchor phrase avoids stock photo look

test_cases.description: True crime podcast — dark but not exploitative, narrative journalism quality
test_cases.input_summary: Dark aesthetic required, but client wants to avoid exploitation aesthetics; tension between dark subject and ethical presentation
test_cases.output_summary: Visual position statement addresses the ethical tension directly; references cite crime documentary photography (not sensationalist media) as anchors; 'what this is not' section explicitly names exploitative true crime imagery conventions to avoid; AI anchor phrase includes 'no graphic imagery, no crime scene staging'

notes: Fluency is the strongest dimension — the mood board brief writes at a genuinely evocative level, not just a descriptive one. The five-dimension structure in prose (not bullets) is the key quality differentiator. The skill generates useful reference suggestions even when the user hasn't provided them.
